injuries: Caleb Foster (foot fracture, out until Final Four)
Patrick Ngongba (foot soreness, expected back)
scout_profile.notes: Nation's best team. Freshman-heavy but elite talent. 11-game win streak.

injuries: Jaylin Stewart (uncertain, missed Big East tournament)
scout_profile.notes: Back-to-back champion seeking three-peat. Slightly down year but still elite.

injuries: Divine Ugochukwu (season-ending injury, no backup PG)
scout_profile.notes: Izzo's tournament pedigree. Senior-led, grind-it-out. 28th straight tournament.

injuries: Darryn Peterson (missed 11 games, star freshman)
scout_profile.notes: Bill Self's tournament machine. Down year by KU standards.
Rick Pitino's St. John's resurgence. Guard-dominated attack. Big East champion.

injuries: Mikel Brown Jr. (lower back, trying to return for NCAAs)
scout_profile.notes: Kelsey year 2. Good energy but tournament wins elusive (0-6 career).

injuries: Tyler Bilodeau (out/limited, leading scorer)
Donovan Dent (calf injury, limited)
scout_profile.notes: Mick Cronin's gritty defense. Big Ten battle-tested.

injuries: Brandon Noel (foot, out since January)
Taison Chatman (groin, day-to-day)
scout_profile.notes: Jake Diebler's first NCAA tournament. Promoted from interim.
Dixon's veteran TCU squad. Hot into the tournament with 8-2 last 10.

injuries: Jamichael Stillwell (ankle)
John Bol (chest, day-to-day)
scout_profile.notes: Dawkins in year 10. Multiple injuries cloud outlook.
First tournament in 36 years. First-year HC after Amir Abdur-Rahim's passing. 9-game win streak.
Jacobson's 20th year. MVC tourney champion as 6-seed. Classic 12-seed upset threat.
First D1 tournament ever. Croy coached through D2 transition. Dominique Daniels Jr. 23.2 ppg.
Richman's 11th year. School-record wins. Summit League champion.
SoCon champion. Alex Wilkins 34 pts in SoCon semifinal. 2023 upset of Virginia as 13-seed.
MAAC champion. McNamara (Syracuse legend) in first tournament as HC.

injuries: Micah Handlogten (medical redshirt, out for season)
Thomas Haugh (foot, expected back)
scout_profile.notes: Defending champions. Golden's best team. Nation's best AdjEM.

injuries: J'Wan Roberts (ankle, expected to play)
scout_profile.notes: Elite defense (nation's best). Sampson's most experienced squad. Big 12 runner-up.

injuries: Keaton Wagler (back spasms)
scout_profile.notes: Underwood year 9. Blew double-digit lead in Big Ten tourney. Still dangerous.

injuries: Rienk Mast (illness, hospitalized before Big Ten tourney)
scout_profile.notes: Hoiberg's best Nebraska season. 24-game win streak broken late. Big Ten QF exit.

injuries: Duke Miles (knee, missed 6 games but returned)
scout_profile.notes: SEC tourney run — beat Florida and Tennessee. Byington building fast at Vanderbilt.

injuries: Caleb Wilson (broken thumb, out for season — leading scorer, projected top-5 pick)
scout_profile.notes: Inconsistent after losing Caleb Wilson (broken thumb, out). UNC always dangerous in March.
Bennett's system: deliberate, precise, senior-led. 25th year. Always dangerous.

injuries: Carter Welling (torn ACL, out for season — 2nd scorer, top rebounder)
Dillon Hunter (broken hand, out)
scout_profile.notes: Brownell's 16th year. Lost starting F (Welling, ACL) and key G (Hunter, hand) in ACC tourney.
McCollum's 1st year at Iowa (from Drake). 4 D2 championships at NW Missouri State. Cold entering tourney.
McMillan year 1 (from Samford). SEC battle-tested but early SEC tourney exit.
HAVOC defense reborn. A-10 champion. Martelli Jr. year 1 (from Bryant). 11-seed danger zone.
Armstrong year 1 (promoted after Will Wade left for NC State). 10-0 last 10. 12-seed upset threat.
Cross's 7th year. 2nd straight Sun Belt title. Troy's program is legit.

injuries: Ethan Roberts (concussion, trying to return for NCAAs)
scout_profile.notes: McCaffery year 1 at Penn (from Iowa). Won Ivy tourney in OT. TJ Power leads the way.
First tournament since 1990. Pribble won 4 games in 5 days in Big Sky tourney.
SWAC champion. 7-game winning streak. First Four vs Lehigh.
Patriot League champion. Reed's 19th year. 6-game winning streak. First Four vs PVAMU.
May year 2. Deep guard corps. Big Ten runner-up. FAU Final Four pedigree.
Elite defense, efficient offense. Big 12 semifinal exit. Otzelberger's best squad.
Post-Bennett era. ACC runner-up (lost to Duke in final). Sanchez's system producing results.
Most 3PA rate in tournament. Feast or famine offense. SEC quarterfinal exit.
Texas Tech defense DNA. Slow pace, elite D. McCasland year 3.

injuries: Nate Ament (knee/ankle, returned for SEC tourney)
scout_profile.notes: Barnes year 11. Rugged SEC defense. SEC QF exit to Vanderbilt.

injuries: Jayden Quaintance (out since Jan 7, will NOT play opening weekend)
Jaland Lowe (out for season)
Malachi Moreno (leg, questionable)
scout_profile.notes: Pope year 2. Talented but decimated by injuries. Classic upset risk as 7-seed.
White year 4. SEC-toughened. Solid 8-seed.
Schertz year 2 (from Indiana State). 28-5 record. Dangerous 9-seed with system play.
Sendek year 10. WCC runner-up. Beat Saint Mary's in semis. Underrated 10-seed.
Enfield year 2 in ACC (from USC). First Four matchup vs Miami OH.
Steele year 4. MAC contender. First Four matchup vs SMU.
Groce's experience. MAC champion. 12-seed upset threat vs Texas Tech.
Claxton year 3. CAA champion. Former NBA PG brings defensive intensity.
Horizon League champion. Sargent's experienced squad.
OVC champion. Beat Morehead State 93-67 in final.
America East champion. The 2018 16-over-1 upset school returns. First Four vs Howard.
MEAC champion. Blakeney building Howard hoops culture. First Four vs UMBC.

injuries: Jaden Bradley (left wrist, taped but playing)
scout_profile.notes: Lloyd year 5. Big 12 champion over Houston. Elite offensive efficiency.
Painter year 21. Big Ten tourney champion — 4 wins in 4 days as 7-seed. Veteran squad.

injuries: Braden Huff (dislocated kneecap, out until Sweet 16 at earliest)
Jalen Warley (thigh contusion, limited)
scout_profile.notes: Few year 27. WCC champion. But Braden Huff (kneecap) out until Sweet 16.

injuries: Darius Acuff Jr. (ankle, returned for SEC tourney)
scout_profile.notes: Calipari year 2. SEC champion — first for Arkansas in 26 years. Hot into the tournament.
Elite shot selection, deliberate pace. Senior-led. Clutch FT shooting.
Young year 2. NBA-style spacing. Big 12 quarterfinal exit.
Lucas year 1 (first HC job after Jim Larranaga retired). 17-win improvement. First tournament.
Willard year 1 at Nova (from Maryland). Big East QF upset loss to Georgetown.
Calhoun year 2. MWC double champion (regular + tourney). 10-game win streak.
Gates year 4. SEC-toughened. Close-game experience.
Miller year 1 at Texas (from Xavier). SEC bubble team. First Four vs NC State.
Wade year 1 at NC State (from McNeese). 2024 Final Four was under Kevin Keatts. First Four vs Texas.
Clayman year 1 (promoted after Huss departed). 30-4 season. 12-seed upset threat.
Ganot year 11. First Big West title in a decade. First tourney since 2016.
Pettway year 3. CUSA champion. First-ever CUSA title for Kennesaw.
Historic: first D1 tournament ever for Queens program. ASUN champion in OT.
Strickland year 4. Former NBA star. NEC champion and Coach of the Year.
